After contacting support and requesting a refund for not giving 24 hours notice because of a billing issue and not for my own convenience I was charged $129 to my credit card by included health and denied a refund even though they claim to allow forgiveness for up to one late notice or missed appointment per year under certain circumstances. They denied me a refund despite billing issues causing the problem in the first place. I called support to appeal the  decision to deny me a refund and they said there’s nothing they can do because you can only submit a claim ticket once. Apparently included health considers the patient at fault for billing issues they admit were the fault of BCBS of Texas. Included Health isn’t transparent and couldn’t even tell me if the cancelled appointment was picked up by another patient or not. They have charged my credit card $129 without allowing me to request a review or reversal of their decision to deny me a refund

I wish I could give a negative review. I wish I could give a negative review, not just one star.  This is just an outright scam.  I was supposed to receive an email with a way to enter the appointment at 11:40 AM.  No email.  Yet at 11:45 AM, I did receive an email from them (so confirmation that they had my correct email) informing me that I missed the appointment, I would be charged and if charge didn't go through it would be turned over to a collections company.  I responded that I didn't receive the appointment email so don't charge me, that my blood pressure was 216/120 and gave them my phone.  No phone call or email back.  So no concern if I die as long as they get $30.  Special place in Hell for these folks.  Avoid at all costs

Employer switched coverage but IH app did not reflect change. My employer switched coverage. Included Health would not automatically update the information in our app to reflect the new coverage without each family member (5 of us) calling them to get the data in the app corrected to the new coverage. I am really not sure what the point is of the service if they have no interest in providing accurate data in the app. As we all know, they want you to use the app and not call them yet they don't want to provide accurate information there without calling them

Dangerously incompetent care program – shocking lack of accountability. Included Health’s new care program is a serious downgrade that puts infants at risk. What used to be access to a pediatrician has been quietly replaced with registered nurses, and the consequences are alarming.
For our one-month-old baby, a nurse advised us to wait 48 hours instead of seeking urgent care. For a newborn. This advice was reckless and completely inappropriate. Had we listened, the outcome could have been disastrous. We ignored the guidance, went to urgent care, and confirmed that waiting would have been a mistake.
To make matters worse, I escalated the issue and spoke with Jennifer from the Member Resolution Team. Rather than acknowledging the severity of the situation, the response felt dismissive and defensive, as if this level of care was acceptable. It is not. A “resolution team” that cannot recognize how dangerous this advice was has no credibility.
Downgrading pediatric care to nurse-only check-ins without clearly warning parents is irresponsible. Parents trust this service during stressful, high-stakes moments — especially with newborns — and Included Health has shown that trust is not deserved.
This program is unsafe, poorly managed, and completely misaligned with basic standards of pediatric care. We will never use this service again, and I strongly warn other parents: do not rely on Included Health for infant medical decisions

Plan $15 and they charged $159, the support call took a lifetime.. Update Feb 17: they sent me a bill for $159!

———
My Plan clearly is that I pay $15 / visit in my network. Any type of doctors: primary, specialist, urgent... All is $15 out of pocket. 

The quote suddenly changed to $130 (from $15), for a 10 mins call... after the appointment. This has to be illegal.

When I tried to reach out to the service support for Billings and Claims, they didn't understand what I was saying and the conversation went in circles. So then they said it could be becase it wasn't a primary care appointment, which it was supposed to be so thats on them... But it doesn't matter, all my appointments are supposed to be $15 and nothing more. Why keep talking about anything but what needs to be fixed... 

I am not going to be using included health any longer, at this point I need to make sure I don't get scammed. So hopefully they resolve this.  Hopefully they do call back or message
Requested note. Requested note, didn't get it, didn't get a refund...stay away they are a scam@

Incompetence and discriminatory behavior.. They spent half month looking for a provider that treats autism / ADHD / PTSD for an adult male...  They pat themselves on the back during the process giving you this false sense that they are going to find you the ultimate in care.  On their app while you're waiting it says they're using billions of data points to calculate the best possible provider..   so what did they find in autistic man a half month later? Which by the way I have the screenshots to prove it. 

They found me a gynecologist... Yep...   After I pointed this out they did an investigation and apologized..  there's so many times that they failed. They sent me a email stating that my diagnosis is complex and difficult. And I am to no longer use their app. But only use their email.. 

And within that email I have a minimum of a 3-day wait for any replies instead of an instantaneous reply using the app.. then they limited my access to emails to 5 days a week. When all my other employees have 24/7 access..  the more they try to help me the more they exposed how truly incompetent and untrained they are..  but in the process of providing extreme limits on me. The state of Pennsylvania decided this was a discriminatory..  unequal access to care..  then they tried to cover their tracks by blocking my access to my HPI.. which had the proof that they found me a gynecologist.. m

But once again they violated laws and were reminded to reinstate my HPI or there will be consequences.. and so they did..  I've been with my company for 25 years. We've had many healthcare type services like this in the past. Included health is by far the most incompetent I have ever dealt with..    

When they deemed working with an autistic person to be difficult they try to make up rules in their terms of service limiting in my access to their care. This was mention to me in a letter... M

Once again, it was uncovered through an investigation that this terms of service limitation didn't even exist they created it to try to get a person with disabilities to not bother them. Because they legitimately did not know what they were doing....   Then they tried to blame me for not using their virtual care through the included health app. I had to remind them they do not provide autism related care. So after they were done blaming me for not taking advantage of the resources now they didn't want to talk about that anymore because they realized they just made a fool out of themselves in writing.. 

By blaming me for not taking advantage of virtual care that they don't even offer... They are so bad if I didn't have all this stuff in print. It would look like the biggest fabrication..  but the fact that I do have all this in print is why there is still a pending State investigation. 

They discriminated against me so badly that they introduced me to a new term I wasn't familiar with. Gaslighting. They would gaslight me and tell me they're not discriminating against me. And they're not limiting my access to their care.  As they put in writing that I am only allowed to contact them through email. As they are disabling the chat feature and the reasons for being this way is they do not have people clinically trained to help me through the app..  I also have screenshots of that statement.. 

The worst is when they lie to an autistic person with adhd. Gaslight them. Make them feel like a horrible person for needing help beyond what the average person needs. Labels them difficult and complex with their health care needs. And then makes it increasingly difficult and time consuming. Where in one situation they took 35 days to get back to me..  despite the fact that they said they're not limiting my access..  

At this point I told them to deactivate my account. The investigation is still going on. But I told them I will not allow you to treat me this way. I would rather get no care. Then dysfunctional care that resents that people like me exist..  I have so many screenshots of everything that they've said.  ... I never in my life imagine somebody with health in their name, looking at an autistic person with ADHD as a burden, as complex, as difficult, and as something they would rather not deal with

Four out of four times bad experience. Used them fourth times. First provider gave me a lecture how we all learned on intro to med school just bacteria does not cause pain in lymph nodes and to take ibuprofen. When I pointed out that I’m breast feeding (which we in my intake questionnaire and I had already mentioned) and can’t take ibuprofen, he laughed (not a nice laugh) and said: then good luck! And hung up. I could only see his mouth but his entire face. 
Second time, said they’d call in a med for acute condition keeping me from going to work. 5 hours later prescription was not issues. I missed an additonal day at work bc I couldn’t get meds in time due to delay.
Third time I called for a UTI, but provider wanted to talk about why breast feeding is not going well. I was seeing my doctor, child’s doctor and lactation specialist. I needed help with the UTI not breast feeding.
Fourth time was for my child. The provider could see us but we couldn’t see her. Somehow she still blamed us for not being able to see her and didn’t even try to correct the issue.  Two days later and I’m still trying to get a doctors note for school that supposedly was going to be issued immediately. They finally wrote one and said I hadn’t given consent for information. I did, but they kept asking me to what the diagnosis was but the provider didn’t give us one, just said to keep child home to rest. Plus a diagnosis doesn’t even go on a doctors note for school. The letter looks fake, signature looks like a child’s and the statement that I won’t consent to release of information not only is not true but makes my sound as though there was no real illness.

Three out of three bad experiences. Fortunately, my insurance pays for a different Tele health provider as well. Never need to use this one again

I've just spent the last week chasing down an order for imaging after speaking with a provider last week.  It took numerous long phone calls and being placed on hold while agents talked to another department, being asked the same questions about the facility it needed to be sent to, frustrating waits while new agents reviewed what was going on, questions that made absolutely no sense...As a provider myself, I cannot tell you how disappointed I am in this service and how much time this company wasted of my life in the last week.  I've been awake since early this morning after just a few hours of sleep trying to track down the imaging order because my appointment is now this morning. No added value, and their service keeps worsening over time. My insurance used to include a benefit called Grand Rounds, where a team would recommend doctors and schedule appointments for me. Unfortunately, ever since they rebranded as Included Health, they got rid of this concierge service, and now just provide lists of doctors (sometimes inaccurate), which I could have gotten just by going to my insurance website myself.

Worse than that, I'm forced to get my mental health care through Included Health's Doctor on Demand video call feature, which is a nightmare when their website and app keep crashing right before a scheduled video call. I logged in 20 minutes before a call today but the website provided no way to log in to the call, and in fact erased my entire video call history and care team.

They used to provide a customer service phone number, but that's vanished from the website too; looks like they laid off a lot of their customer service reps. Instead I had to go through an AI chatbot, which told me I would get a response "within 24 hours." Not helpful when my scheduled call was in 15 minutes.

I only barely avoided the $95 no-show fee by downloading the app at the last minute.

But after the call, the app would crash every single time I tried to book a follow-up visit with my doctor. And the website still removed every possible way to book a follow-up visit with him. So I have to wait until they fix their app or website to see my doctor, I guess

Don't Provide Claimed Service, Spammers. Their service is unhelpful and they are incorrigible spammers. They are offered as a free benefit through my husband's employer, so we decided to try them. Even under life-threatening, St. Joseph does not permit abortions in their hospitals, and they do not allow doctors to refer patients for abortions. We will not support any health system them puts religious views over healtcare or any patient's life. Included Health referred both of us to different doctors affiliated with this system (my husband didn't find out until his gastroenterologist, who had a separate office outside of the hospital, schedule a colonoscopy at St. Joseph Hospital where he also practices). I discovered the affiliation of the doctor they referred me to before scheduling, and I sent them links to his profile on the St. Joseph website, but in spite of this evidence, they proceeded to try to gaslight me about his affiliation. In both cases, these doctors were just one of the first to pop up in the list of in-network providers on our insururer's websites and had no particular claims to being LGBT friently suggesting they put in no dilligence at all in their referrals

The other caregivers lied and said they did laundry for 4 hours, and management never did anything, but they seemed to penalize me for not completing a task that went undone. In addition, I was given assignments outside my area, traveling 44.0 miles to Saratoga and 29.7 miles to Daly City (28.7 miles) + bridge toll fees. Confusing cancellation process and extremely frustrating customer service. Title: Confusing cancellation process and extremely frustrating customer service

I signed up for Care.com years ago but had not meaningfully used the service in a very long time. After being charged for a renewal previously, I believed I had canceled my subscription. I later discovered that I was charged another annual renewal fee of $165.65.

When I logged in to cancel again, I realized the cancellation process requires multiple screens, confirmations, and additional prompts before cancellation is actually finalized. The workflow is confusing enough that it is very easy to believe your subscription has already been canceled when it has not.

As soon as I noticed the charge, I immediately canceled the account and contacted customer service requesting a refund. Unfortunately, most of the responses I received were scripted replies repeating that subscriptions are “non refundable,” without meaningfully addressing the cancellation experience itself.

The customer service process became frustrating on its own. When I tried replying to support emails, I repeatedly received automated messages saying the issue had already been resolved and directing me back into the Help Center workflow to start over again. Eventually, my newer support requests stopped receiving responses altogether.

What concerned me most was discovering during my research that the FTC had previously taken enforcement action against Care.com related to confusing subscription cancellation practices and consumers continuing to be billed after believing they had canceled. I had no prior knowledge of that case before researching my own issue, but the concerns described by the FTC closely matched my experience.

This entire situation could have been resolved with a simple goodwill refund for a service I did not knowingly intend to renew and had not meaningfully used in years.

Care.com could improve this experience significantly by:

* simplifying and clarifying the cancellation process,
* providing clearer confirmation when cancellation is complete,
* and empowering customer service to reasonably resolve unintended renewal situations instead of relying entirely on scripted responses.

Update after Care.com's response:

The company's response to this review unfortunately illustrates one of the central issues I described above.

After detailing the confusing cancellation experience, the renewal dispute, the repeated scripted customer service interactions, the FTC enforcement action that raised similar concerns, and the months spent attempting to resolve the matter, Care.com's response was simply a generic statement that they were "sorry to hear" about my experience and that they would "share the feedback with the appropriate teams."

Notably, the response does not address a single issue raised in the review. It does not address the cancellation process, the renewal dispute, the customer service experience, the refusal to provide a goodwill refund, or the concerns regarding the FTC's prior findings.

Prospective customers should understand that if they encounter a billing or cancellation issue, they may receive the same type of scripted, non-specific response rather than a meaningful attempt to address the underlying concern.

At this point, my concern is no longer the $165.65 charge itself. It is the apparent unwillingness to engage substantively with legitimate customer concerns, even after multiple escalations and public reviews

There were very few reviews for carers in my area, which made it difficult to assess people properly and use the platform with confidence. The website was also slow, awkward to navigate and did not meet the standard I would expect from a paid premium service.

Most disappointing was the lack of effective customer support. When I wanted to request a refund, it was extremely difficult to contact anyone. The live chat did not work, there was no obvious monitored support email, and the help centre was too generic to be genuinely helpful.

Overall, I found Care.com poor value for money, difficult to use and lacking the level of customer support and transparency that a service of this nature should provide.

They were understanding of my concerns, and were willing to process the refund as a one-off exception. They have fed back some of my development ideas/technical issues, although I'm sure future users will see whether these actually get implemented. I Used to Love Care.com. Care.com was a wonderful business that was founded in the early aughts by a smart woman, whose focus seemed to be providing excellent service and having happy customers.  I remember in its early days, it felt like the people who ran and worked for the company really cared about helping customers who needed services find good service providers nearby.  So, the business was successful, it had an IPO and was ultimately acquired by IAC, a large company that has a sizeable global portfolio of media and digital businesses that generate ~$2.4B/year.  As part of this holding company, Care.com's service has deteriorated.  Now, when I post a job, I receive what feel like auto-generated applications--i.e., the so-called "applicants" haven't even read my job description/requirements, so they don't realize that they're not a fit. Sometimes, they're not even in my geographic area.  Plus, I suspect that many of them wouldn't have applied if they had actually read my job description.  Sending me auto-generated applications is a total waste of my time and very annoying.   I also don't like the fact that IAC converts you to an auto-renewing membership if you want to be able to communicate with applicants.  Who the heck is going to hire a nanny, housekeeper or dogwalker without communicating with them?   Bottom line: What used to be a nice, useful personal service business, now feels like a completely impersonal and scammy money-making SaaS platform machine.  No like

I really don't know how they advertise their website, but my web developer looks like doing a much better job in advertising, because we show up on the first page, with very little budget, Care.com on the second page or later.

For a paid service, the value simply isn’t there. You’re essentially paying for access to a database that is outdated and unresponsive. The platform does little to ensure engagement or accountability from caregivers, which defeats the purpose of using a service like this in the first place.

I’ve had significantly better luck using community-based platforms like Nextdoor and local neighborhood groups, where people posting are actually active and interested in helping. The difference in responsiveness and quality is night and day.

Care.com banned my family to protect a caregiver who stole from us. I want to share my experience so other families know what they’re signing up for.
We hired a caregiver through Care.com who quit after two weeks via a hostile text message accusing us of being bad parents because we declined her developmentally inappropriate and unsolicited advice about our infant. We later discovered she had also stolen a personal item from our home. Shortly after her departure, our account was permanently terminated with no explanation.
When I asked what happened, Care.com told me they are not required to tell me what I was accused of, who accused me, or what evidence exists. Their appeals process requires you to log into an account that has been closed — so it doesn’t actually function. Their own customer service confirmed they don’t have access to the specifics of why accounts are closed. I submitted evidence showing the caregiver’s retaliatory motive. It was ignored.
Over the course of five responses through the Better Business Bureau, Care.com sent the same copy-pasted reply every single time without engaging with a single point I raised. I have also filed complaints with the Federal Trade Commission and my state Attorney General’s office.
So to be clear: the caregiver who quit in a hostile manner and stole from my home is still on the platform. My family is banned. And because my account is closed, I can’t even leave a review to warn other families about her. Care.com sees no problem with any of this.
This is a company that will take your money, allow someone to file an anonymous report against you with no verification, permanently ban you with no explanation, and then tell you repeatedly that their policy says they don’t have to do anything about it.
If you are considering Care.com to find care for your children, your parents, or anyone you love, please think carefully. This company has already paid $8.5 million to settle FTC charges for deceptive practices, paid $1 million to California prosecutors for misrepresenting background checks, and was the subject of a Wall Street Journal investigation that found caregivers with criminal records committing crimes against children in their care.
Care has never factored into anything this company does

Misleading pricing and disappointing experience. In my experience, Care.com did not meet my expectations.

The platform advertises hourly rates for services like cleaning, but in practice, most providers I contacted did not offer hourly pricing. Instead, they quoted fixed prices that were significantly higher than expected, without first clarifying my needs.

For example, I was quoted $500 for a cleaning service, even though I had not requested a deep clean. This made the experience feel inconsistent with what is presented on the platform.

I also reached out to customer support to explain the situation. While I initially received a response, I did not receive any follow-up after my reply, which I found frustrating.

Overall, based on my experience, I did not find the platform useful for my needs.

For childcare, I personally found alternatives like Sitting Made Simple to be more structured.

For cleaning services, I would suggest considering established cleaning companies, where staff are typically insured, background-checked, and service expectations are clearer.

This review reflects my personal experience using the platform

Backup care was useless in my experience. I recently tried using Care.com for backup care, including both daycare and in-home options, and unfortunately my experience was not very positive.

For daycare backup care, the stated policy mentions that requests should be approved or declined within 48 hours. However, in my case, I waited about five days without a clear decision. During that time, I only received emails saying they were still searching for a provider, which made it difficult to plan accordingly.

I then tried requesting in-home backup care and selected a provider through the platform. The request status showed as “confirmed,” which was reassuring at first. However, according to the guidelines, the caregiver is supposed to reach out at least one day before the scheduled start date. I did not receive any communication, which made the “confirmed” status feel misleading.

Additionally, it seems that some of the caregivers listed on the platform may not be actively available. If a caregiver’s schedule does not show any availability, it may indicate that they are not currently taking assignments. In my experience, even with a “confirmed” request, the lack of visible availability was a strong indication that the caregiver had not personally confirmed the assignment, making the “confirmed” status appear misleading.

Overall, the lack of timely updates, clear communication, and accurate availability made it hard to depend on the service when I needed it. While the platform has potential, my experience suggests there may be gaps in coordination and follow-through that could be improved

Immediately I cancelled again via the website, and it showed that my account would be "downgraded to free" at the end of the billing cycle ... but it also still showed a future billing date and the "manage membership" button still took me to a page that showed an ACTIVE subscription!! It allowed me to go through this PHONY cancellation process as many times as I wanted! I contacted customer support about the issue and all they said was "we don't do refunds" and basically saying I knew that I was signing up for auto-renewals when I signed up. So after multiple attempts with customer support and finally submitting one last angry complaint via their stupid portal, I went ahead and disputed the charges through my credit card company. I had mentioned in my final complaint that I would do this. The next morning my account had been wiped and it APPEARS that they issued the refunds (after saying they wouldn't). When I called my credit card to remove the disputes they so kindly advised me to wait until the refunds actually get posted on the account, because right now they are just pending. The credit card company said many scammy companies do this - they'll "issue a refund" so it shows up in pending transactions just to get you to drop your charge disputes, but those refunds will drop off and never actually post to your account!!! We'll see what happens but I would not be surprised AT ALL if that's how this shakes out. Care.com is so obviously scammy it's insane. IDK how on earth they're the main provider for connecting parents & nannies

The FTC is taking action against this company for running a scam, and I'm not surprised. As a caregiver and child development professional, I'd been using Care.com for 10 years. I have watched the platform evolve from a robust job finding tool into a mostly-broken app filled with hollow job postings and constant cash grabs. Want to respond to messages? Got to be a paying member. But the family has to pay also...you can send the messages, but if you want to read 'em you gotta pay and so do they! Very scammy. Additionally, families who join the platform automatically have a job posting created for them as part of the registration, which clogs up the actual job listings with "We're looking now for a nanny in [location] who is CPR certified and has college degree", posts from families who often don't even realize they have a job posting because it doesn't notify them. So of course, if you take time and apply to these jobs, you never get an answer, and I never applied to them anyway because it was a clear waste of my time, plus how do I know it's a good fit for me when there are zero details about the kids, what they like, their ages, etc?

The messaging component and review component are both barely functioning. I regularly got notifications that I had messages or "job requests" that didn't exist. "Job requests" are actually a scam in my opinion, as often times the app would send a notification which made it seem like a family messaged you inquiring about your availability, when in fact they had not and the message was automated. I had a family hire me off one of these who clearly explained that they never reached out, the message was automated and completely un-requested by them. So the Care.com app is pretending to be your family and messaging caregivers for you? A bit creepy.  When a real human did send the message, it often didn't arrive, or when it did arrive it arrived days later. Notifications were delayed as well, and some job requests expired after 24 hours, so by the time you got a notification, the request wasn't available anymore. Not to mention they penalized you in suarch standings for your reply speed, but unless you check messages every 30 minutes you'd miss one because notifications were so broken.
I had families write me reviews and they never showed up. I had families go to write reviews and get told they couldn't be submitted because they already wrote me one when they didn't. Again, when reviews are so important to search rankings, this level of dysfunction is inexcusable as it actively hurts your chances of being hired.

So overall, the app is broken and borders on a large-scale scam, messaging works poorly, reviews are broken, and ethics at corporate appear to be non-existent. You pay money for a service but the service is almost unusable. The FTC has actually taken action against whis company for their scams in the past and I'd love to see them take further action in the future. You'd be better served putting up posters on telephone poles looking for childcare or a caregiver and stapling the money below the sign. You're throwing money away and getting nothing in return either way

Predatory Business Practices. If I could give 0 stars, I would. I signed up for what I believed was a one-month subscription and did not realize it would automatically renew every three months. I stopped using the service shortly after joining because I was unable to find reliable care through the platform.

What was most frustrating is that I never received any email notification that my membership was renewing or that another charge was about to occur. Most subscription services send a reminder before a renewal, especially for a charge of this size. By the time I noticed the charge on my credit card, I had been billed $600.

I contacted customer support right away to explain the situation, but was told that their customer agreement does not allow refunds.

I understand companies need policies, but the lack of renewal notification combined with the inability to issue any refund for a service that wasn’t being used feels very disappointing and not particularly customer-friendly. I hope the company will reconsider how they handle renewals and customer support in situations like this.

Misleading Subscription Model and Lack of Transparency. Care.com is misleading. It presents itself as a marketplace similar to Thumbtack, but it is not. You are required to create an account just to search, and only after doing so do you discover that you must pay a significant subscription fee simply to contact a provider. You are paying merely to send a message to someone who may or may not ever clean your house.

What makes it worse is that providers can contact you, which creates the impression that you can respond freely. In reality, you cannot reply unless you are paying. That wastes everyone’s time and creates unnecessary confusion. 

Some providers here complain about “fake jobs.” They aren’t fake. It’s Care.com’s shady marketing practices. We CANNOT reply. They shouldn’t send our info to you if we don’t join. 

In August 2024, Care.com agreed to an approximately $8.5 million settlement with the Federal Trade Commission over allegations of deceptive claims about job availability and earnings and making it difficult for users to cancel subscriptions. The company did not admit wrongdoing, but the issues raised in that case reflect the same lack of transparency that users still experience.

Costs and subscription requirements should be clearly disclosed before someone creates an account and certainly before people begin messaging one another

Care.com is undoubtedly one of the best…. Care.com is undoubtedly one of the best platforms for connecting caregivers with families, and it has created many valuable opportunities for people in the caregiving industry.

However, one concern I have is that caregivers often do not feel they have an equal voice on the platform. In situations where a caregiver is treated unfairly or receives an inaccurate review, there appears to be NO support or resolution available. At the same time, families are able to leave public reviews that can significantly impact a caregiver’s reputation and future opportunities.

Caregivers are just as important as the families they serve, and there should be a balanced review system that allows caregivers to also provide honest feedback about the families they have worked with. Creating a fair and transparent environment for both parties would strengthen trust and accountability across the platform.

I’m a certified Health Care Aide. I’m a certified Health Care Aide, and I make $18.50 an hour. When I look at how close that is to minimum wage — especially with minimum wage going up again in April — it’s hard not to feel the gap between the work I do and the value placed on it. I care for people at their most vulnerable moments, physically and emotionally, and I give my whole heart to this work, yet my wage doesn’t reflect the weight of that responsibility.

At Home Instead, there is also no paid overtime and no benefits, which makes that gap even more obvious. There was also a statement from the owner that any kilometres driven outside of Red Deer would be paid at 75 cents per kilometre, yet no one receives this either. Many Health Care Aides across Alberta are going through the same situation — doing essential, hands‑on care for wages that barely sit above minimum wage.

The office sees the hard work we do every day, from acute care to hospice care, yet the support we need isn’t reflected in wages, policies, or follow‑through. I previously worked in a hotel making $18 an hour just to make beds and clean — tasks I also do in this job — yet here I carry far more responsibility, emotional labour, and risk for nearly the same pay.

I care so deeply for my clients that it’s not in me to just walk away for a higher‑paying job. I don’t want my clients stressed or left wondering if I’m coming back. Many of them would ask over and over where I went, or worry that something was wrong. I could get a job with the City making close to $30 an hour just to take care of the outdoors, but my clients are number one to me — and that loyalty should matter.

Home Instead also has an Employee of the Month program, but because I work 12‑hour shifts Monday to Friday, I’m not eligible since I can’t work weekends. I work 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. just to cover my $3000 rent and bills, living paycheck to paycheck, needing a car to get to work, and trying to keep up with gas, food, and everything else life demands. On weekends, I clean houses so I can afford to put my daughter in softball. That level of commitment should be recognized, not excluded.

All of us employees need to stand together, and the office manager should be supporting their staff instead of leaving them to struggle alone. Being paid only slightly above minimum wage highlights a serious issue within the company and within the healthcare system as a whole: the people providing intimate, essential care are not compensated in a way that matches the skill, training, and emotional labour required. Sustainable, quality care depends on fair wages, fair policies, and fair treatment for frontline workers.

I want to give this company a higher…. I want to give this company a higher rating, and I do not like to write bad reviews against companies. Honor had a good schedule mobile app that was convenient for picking up shifts and writing notes about the activities for your job tasks completed while working. However, I think I was hired as part of a scam since after my hire date (April 1, 2025), I worked 14 hours, and I received my paycheck on 4/10/25, and I am locked out of my portal to access my pay stub. The portal reads another name that says, "Home Instead." This differs from my employee badge, which says "Honor," as seen in the app on my cell phone. I contacted management, but they never responded to help me. In addition, I live in San Leandro. I worked in Berkeley on an assignment for an older woman who was kind and felt disappointed with this company because they send people who did not know how to operate her washing machine and dryer and her laundry was never washed for a couple of weeks until I arrived, I operated the washing machine and dryer without any problems. The other caregivers lied and said they did laundry for 4 hours, and management never did anything, but they seemed to penalize me for not completing a task that went undone. In addition, I was given assignments outside my area, traveling 44.0 miles to Saratoga and 29.7 miles to Daly City (28.7 miles) + bridge toll fees. I was not paid for gas mileage or bridge toll fees, and the job assignments offered closer to my location within 15.5 miles were cancelled. I was told the client had cancelled, and I had to accept at least two job assignments, or I would be fired. I'm not feeling well about this company, I told them I feel sick, and now all my job assignments have been cancelled and put on hold for one day until I feel better and complete a survey online to remove the hold I never heard from management when I asked if the survey could be mailed by USPS or sent to my email so I can print out a hard copy and send it to my doctor then send back to them, Management said, "Hi Briggette, we are unable to send this form via USPS" I never want to do business with them again, because it's all online business and doesn't allow for more non-verbal cues, immediate feedback, and stronger personal connections

Shame on them - bereavement skills non existent!. The carers are wonderful, the admin staff and company are another story.  My father died 15 minutes before the night carer arrived.  She called Home Instead straight away to advise them.  We asked her to stay for an around an hour with us while they came to verify his death. No message of condolence, or even an email or phone call acknowledging that he had died. No contact from them at all until we asked for a bill, and even then it was not an appropriate response which we were extremely disappointed by, we were flabbergasted to be honest.  The office have been so shockingly lacking in bereavement etiquette and care, whereas some of the staff have actually had bereavement training. Many of the wonderful staff are often pushed to the limit, often tired and are told to eat on the run in their cars.  Perhaps the company should save money on their unnecessary free logo mugs and fridge magnets, and put it towards bereavement training for the admin staff which is massively lacking. 5 stars for the carers and 1 star for the admin / office staff.  We dealt with the Bletchley / Milton Keynes office.  Shame on them!

HomeInstead North Wales bad experience.. Despite having an excellent experience with HomeInstead Chester for my elderly mother, we had to use a different franchise as she moved into Wales.  We used HomeInstead (the North Wales branch) for a very short time as she fell within a week and ended up in hospital.  

I found this franchise annoyingly greedy with poor communication with family.  If you are using this company PLEASE read the small print in your contract as not all franchise contracts will be the same.  Despite knowing my mother was in hospital and my keeping them fully informed they still insisted on charging her for full fees for the time she remained in hospital.  We were told we should be grateful that they had made the ‘generous’ offer of not charging for the full month and only 3 weeks! Full payment for doing absolutely nothing. Interestingly if we had remained with Chester they would have only charged for 24 hours. I naively thought both contracts would be very similar, how wrong I was.  Additionally the North Wales branch then wanted to charge 60% of fees just to keep my mother’s care slots!  I’m appalled at the greed of this company and was shocked that the two franchises worked so differently.  READ THE SMALL PRINT if you use Hominstead North Wales

A little heads up. A little heads up, what you can expect, from Home instead, if you go for an interview with them.
This was the Norfolk/Cambridge branch, in the UK.
   So, yesterday I had an online interview with Home Instead, and I thought it went fine, but obviously not, as I didn't get the job.
The young man, said that at the end of the day, he would contact me, he didn't. I had to chase up an answer today. 
It is what it is, not getting the job, that one has applied for, but the reasons given were so completely untrue and unfair. But that just shows, that if a company is  behaving like that, then it is of course, best not to work for them. 

The reasons they gave me was, that I didn't show interest in the work...I was shocked to hear that, as I most definitely showed great interest, in working with the elderly clients. I kept on asking questions, whether it was ok, to do gardening with the clients, and cooking, baking, painting, arts and crafts etc. 
I also asked for advice, in how to deal with challenging situations, if they should arise.
So, I did ask many questions about different scenarios, and also asked if I could do certain activities with them, such as gardening, baking, etc.
If that is not showing interest, and involvement in the job, I don't know what is.

Then they also mentioned, that being dressed in a hoodie, was not considered appropriate and unprofessional...Now this also shocked me, as my hoodie was down, it was obviously NOT over my head, it was down, so my whole head was visible, of course.
I was wearing a clean hoodie, casual relaxed clothing. For this line of work, I would have thought, that this was accepted, as you are to spend, a lot of time, with the elderly client in their homes, so being in comfortable clothing, is a must.
This is not a front of house position, in a corporate business, or in a Hotel. This is looking after elderly clients in their homes, for two weeks at a time, so relaxed clothing should definitely be accepted.
 But clearly it is not.

Another reason, was that I didn't have a fixed address...also not correct. I live in between my caravan, and my art workshop at the moment, while I am looking for a house. However, I have a postal address, where they can reach me, and most importantly, via my phone obviously. 
I will anyway, be away for two weeks at a time, with the client, so those reasons, are completely unfounded.

The only thing, that was close to resembling any truth, was that I didn't know much, about the company, other than it takes care, of elderly people.
 I find this to be so irrelevant, whether you know the whole Wikipedia, about the company, when it was created, every single detail, etc.
They work with elderly people, provide in-house services for them for two weeks at a time, that involving, cooking, gardening, walks in the park, shopping, etc. that should be enough to mention that, and I did.
You may know, all the bio about the company, but still not be a good carer, that doesn't ensure, that you are good at your job. It is about being good with people, and I am.
  it is some weird criteria, and very robotic, the way they judge, whether you are suitable for the position or not.

In this case, the reasons, they gave me, about why, I didn't get the job, was also very untrue and unfounded.

It seems like they are very incompetent rigid and soulless, no passion for engagement with people, nor do they seem to have any skill, in hiring people.
They seemed more interested in the job candidates, knowledge of the company logistics, and how they are dressed, rather than if the job candidate is a warm, caring person, who really would be good as a carer. 

Their reviews speak for themselves, they have 63% one-star reviews and less than 30%, five-star reviews, and that is from both relatives of the clients and people who have worked for them, or been in the onboarding process.
Cold and uncaring people. Would definitely NOT recommend Home instead.

So now you know, what you can expect, from having an interview with Home instead.

                     Karina

This is an update to a previous review…. This is an update to a previous review and one which I am saddened to write. I originally only wanted a one off 24 hours care. The person who I spoke to advised it was better to sign up for a package so the care giver assigned to mum whilst I was away would be familiar with her routine and mum would get to know her. This did not happen. One of The care givers assigned to mum I only met the day before I flew out and I never got to meet the second carer. This meant vital information was not passed on from the carer I met to the one I didn't. Please let me stress the carer I met was lovely and very nice and I know she did her best but mum is incredibly high needs. 98 years. End stage dementia not mobile and a catheter. I was assured at time of signing up that this company specialises in this type of care. Was my mother in mortal danger? No. Could my mothers health been compromised had I been away longer? I believe so. Again everything the carers did was with good intent and I'm sure they were kind and caring to mum but I firmly believe they were not equipped training wise to care for mum. Everything I have said here I have also emailed the company with this so my comments should not be a surprise. I also accept some responsibility. I was so exhausted and stressed I wanted to believe everything they said and I should have asked more questions. Billing was also an issue. I arrived home ,to a broken washing machine, the door to my front loader had been ripped off. No note no apology. But I digress. So on the Saturday I received a message to say that the company had tried to debit my credit card for $5,500 and it had failed. Well yes I wasn't sent an invoice nor advised in advance this amount which is huge for 4 nights was going to be deducted. After much arguing and toing and froing I eventually got the cost of my new machine taken off the invoice. It has ruined my holiday and I'm more stressed than ever. To summarise this is a company. They are not a charitable institution. They are in it to make money and that's fair enough. All I'm asking is please do not make promises that cannot be fulfilled. For everyone else. Ask questions don't take things on face value. I should have stepped up and done my own due diligence which I sorely regret not doing. This company is probably no better or worse than any other. There are many care providers out there. Find the right one for you. I didn't to my regret.
So I am editing this review. I have just seen the credit card amount they deducted to find they have charged me $99 more than the amended invoice I was sent. So guess I'm paying money towards my machine they broke. He very careful of this company. It's extremely sad to think they would amend and deduct extra without advising me. You know they can keep the money. I can put a full stop after this and move on knowing I am an honest person. The same cannot be said for this company

I would reconsider engaging help from this company

SALES MGR PREYED ON OLD AND VULNERABLE GRANDMOTHER. AS PER MY REVIEW FOR HOME INSTEAD UK:
Where to start?!  My late Gran's niece caused the initial mess by sticking her nose in where it wasn't wanted, or needed, and tried to arrange alternative care for my then 95-year-old Gran behind my back (without doing any actual legwork!), even though I was dealing effectively with Gran's care already (and had done for nearly 20 years).  Furthermore, at the time of these events, Gran was being cared for in hospital but immediately prior to this had been in respite care in a care home, since she had ailments that meant she could not live at home and the outlook was permanent care in a care home, NOT home care.  I was Gran's carer/next-of-kin/POA.  The Manager of Live-In Care, Home Instead Glasgow, Stirling, Perth, Ayr and Falkirk (whom we'll call S) started visiting Gran without my knowledge to discuss live-in home care, and I only found out about these visits by chance - just in time too.  S ended up preying on Gran - a very elderly, weak and vulnerable person - directly with unethical and aggressive sales tactics, and I believe this is because Gran told S that she had money that her late husband AND late son had left her, and because she had been deemed as having mental capacity, even though she did not have the capacity to manage her own financial affairs - I dealt with this side of things as her carer/POA.  S hid behind Gran's 'capacity' which became her line of defence.  I had to step in to stop the nonsense before it went too far.  In the end, I sent a MASSIVE complaint email to the Care Inspectorate who passed it to management at Home Instead.  The Group Operations Manager sympathetically acknowledged and apologised that the situation had caused me stress and worry, and though S lied through her teeth about her conduct, he prevented S from continuing to pursue this lead.  In addition, due to my concerns about S, I had to ask that hospital staff, care home staff and the social worker alert me should they find S speaking to or visiting Gran.  SPECIFICS: S did not feel the need to get in touch with Gran's POA/next-of-kin (me), or do a financial assessment, or carry out a risk assessment at Gran's house, or have anyone witness conversations or join in with meetings, nor was she intending to have anyone witness the signing of any forms/agreements (which thankfully never happened but it was close - my Gran couldn't even see the forms!!)  S was hesitant to provide me with information when requested (financial or otherwise), and there was no information left for Gran at the hospital or care home for me to see.  From conversations with her, I don't believe Gran fully understood the costs, what having a live-in carer meant for her and her home, e.g. what service she was to receive and what changes had to be made to her home, and what privacy and security concerns this arrangement could cause.  What information I did finally receive from S was vague.  S was pushing to start the service only a week and a half after I got in touch with her (once I found out what was going on) even though Gran did NOT have an agreed discharge date from the hospital and there was NO agreed plan for her continuing care at that point.  S caused great confusion with hospital staff who thought Gran had home care arranged when she did not.  S even had the gall to ask me for a set of keys to Gran's house and sent me a Direct Debit form to complete!  I was SPEECHLESS.  I'd never even met the woman and I was to let her and who knows who else into Gran's home?!  Plus, as if all of this isn't enough to put people off, it turns out that carers are not always fully qualified to SVQ Level 3 and are often working towards this - therefore you could end up with a 'carer' who started at college yesterday.  Don't worry - you can REQUEST A FULLY QUALIFIED CARER (yes, I was actually told this). 
 Please be very wary of Home Instead and DO YOUR HOMEWORK BEFORE LETTING SOMEONE LIKE 'S' OR AN UNQUALIFIED CARER INTO YOUR FAMILY'S LIFE.   *I have emails to prove the majority of the information I have provided in this review, which were used as evidence for my complaint to the Care Inspectorate.  Other conversations with S were by phone.  None were in person

Shockingly negligent - mother nearly died. Home instead are a shockingly negligent organisation and I do not recommend them.

We pay for 4 visits a day to my disabled mother, including a nighttime visit, to ensure that she is helped into bed each night. Four nights ago, the carer didn't turn up and didn't tell us. My mother tried to get herself into bed and had a fall as a result. We didn't notice until the middle of the night, by which time she'd been on the floor for hours and we had to call an ambulance to help her up as I couldn't reach my sister who lives 10 minutes away. Luckily my sister eventually woke up and was able to go round and help my mother up. The camera in her room saved her life but - shockingly -  Home Instead has reported the camera to social services and had it removed.

What happened was that some of the carers were closing my mother's bedroom door - making it virtually impossible for her to get out of the room in the night with her walker. Other carers were not ensuring she went to the loo at night, meaning she would wake up in a few hours and try to get up to use the loo - she had a couple of falls as a result. Other carers were leaving the light on at night, even though we had asked them to switch it off - my mother would wake up in the night and think it was morning and try to get up - she had a fall as a result.

We noticed all this because of the camera and asked the carers if they would leave the door open, switch off the light and ensure she used the loo before bed. One or more of the carers didn't like that someone was "telling them now to do their job", so they retaliated by complaining that we had a camera in her room and had it removed. 

The worst thing was that they did this without giving us any notice meaning we didn't have time to put other monitoring devices into place. This means that we can no longer see if she's had a fall in the night. So tonight we are not able to look after her properly since they only switched it off at 8pm.

You can reach your own conclusions about this company based on our experiences

This is a terrible company to work for. Loved working with the clients, but company wants to benefit from slave labour to deter staff from leaving. Wants to enslave people by insisting they work for it for a minimum of one year lest you are fined for leaving. What do you call it when you work for a company then they take all the money than an act of slavery? LOL- seeks reimbursement for things you have no choice to opt out of like 'introductions' to their own clients whom they allocate you to work with, training (compulsory), DBS, Digital licence etc. In the 21st century how legal is it for an employer make you a slave who can't leave their employment within 12 months? How 'proportionate' is this? Just because you have offered your services to them, what makes them think they have a right to want to own you for a whole year when you even haven't known if the job is right for you?
This is what their bizarre contract says: 
"Description of Training Cost Venue Provider

1. Introductions and shadowing by a skilled team
member to train to meet client’s specific needs: £20 per Introduction Various Home Instead
2. Follow-up support and training visits with a skilled
team member: £20 per visit Various Home Instead
3. DBS check. £50 N/A Online DBS checker
4. E-learning licence (Care Certificate & amp; Mandatory Training): £99 Digital Platform My Learning Cloud.
5. Digital Inductions & Workshops (Module 1,
Module 2, Module 3, Induction): £100 per session Digital Platform Home Instead.
6. Basic Life Support: £50 Franchise Office Home Instead
7. Moving & Handling £50 Franchise Office Home Instead

The induction training costs amounts to the sum of between £369-£849 inclusive of a DBS check and will be paid for by the Company. I undertake that should I leave my employment with the Company; I will refund to the Company of the aforementioned training costs based on the following criteria:
*You fail to give the company the required contractual notice period in the event you terminate
your employment with the company.
* You terminate your employment with the company within the first 12 months of your
employment.
*The company terminates your employment within your first 12 months of employment for any
reason other than redundancy.
*If changes in your availability have a detrimental impact on working hours being allocated to you,
it will be assumed you are enforcing point 2 above as your contract is based on your availability at
point of application."
They don't tell you this during interviews, or 'training'. Contract or not, this is servitude, and no human being should be subjected to this kind of treatment. In the UK in the 21st century, No human being should be forced to work for a whole year, charged for training which you don't have a right to opt out of even when you already have the training- training that I cannot use anywhere, and for being 'introduced' to the company's  own clients, otherwise they lose all their last month's pay, which is paid in the middle of the following month!
AVOID BY ALL MEANS AND BEWARE of small print by this company OR BE THEIR NEXT VICTIM/forced donor!!!!!!

HOMEINSTEAD- NORTHAMPTON - MOULTON PARK. This is the worst company I have EVER worked for!!! 
I should've seen the red flags before I'd even started. They advertised a full time wage and then got me to sign a zero hour contract. The training was on my birthday and I asked if I could make the next training day because of this in which they responded "no more training days for a while now, you'll have to come to this one or you'll miss out on the job." So ofcourse I went in. Only to find out they were doing training days every week!! As you can imagine I wasn't best pleased. We also got told that we would be paid for both training days. WE DID NOT. 

Okay so moving on to the actual job...
The clients were lovely, Yeah the whole 3 clients I got in an ENTIRE YEAR were lovely. I got promised a full time job and got told I'd have at least 10 clients by the end of month 2 and that they would keep growing until I was on a full wage. This was total bull. Everytime I went in for a meeting I'd bring it up whilst in tears and they'd promise it was going to change. IT DIDN'T! I was really struggling. So I took it on myself to start doing my promo jobs again to fill in the LARGE gaps so I could get by and pay my bills. They would text about 30 minutes before saying they have put me in to see a client because someone else can't make it. (They would do this ALOT) they wouldn't even ask me, they would just put me in and tell me! Sometimes they wouldn't even tell me and then call me asking why I wasn't with the client. So eventually I spoke up about it and explained my situation and that they can't just throw me in last minute without asking me. Not only do I have other jobs booked in now but it's just not on! They were so rude to me and would just hang up the phone and send me snotty messages after. All the nicey nice management turned into really vile rude people. And then all of a sudden I was only doing 2 visits a week which meant I wasn't making any bills. They seemed to be extremely spiteful in the office. 
I'd gone into the office for another catch up meeting and I felt the entire office stare at me and then began talking amongst themselves, clearly about me. I felt extremely uncomfortable so that was the last time I ever worked for the company. 

About the clients and 'paid petrol'...
I got promised paid petrol which was total bull. The 3 clients I had were missions away from my house so this was extremely frustrating and upsetting. 

What was even more upsetting is how little they care about the clients, 2 out of 3 of my clients and the family's of had nothing but bad things to say about the management.
They were so little help in any situation. 
If I was ill with covid symptoms they would tell me that I still had to go and see the clients but I would refuse. 
Another carer was asked to do the same but she didn't refuse and for that her client ended up in the hospital with covid she had transmitted from the carer. 

One of my clients was struggling and was clearly extremely unwell and wasn't at all himself. As a new carer I called the office to ask what I should do (like they asked) their response being "Typical Peter, always something up. Just give him his dinner and put him to bed." 
I didn't, instead I called and ambulance. They had a go saying that I shouldn't of done that and that there was no need. THEN I found out he actually had a really bad infection on his brain. So thank f*** for me and f*** those in the office. 

I could honestly go on and on about all their s*** but I don't have the time. 

All I can say is, if you work for this company or hire them for a family member after reading this, shame on you!

Do not work for this company!. Do not work for this company!

They advertised online for £11.50 weekdays and £13.50 weekends yet when I signed the contract I noticed in my first pay cheque it was actually £1 less for both weekdays and weekends, I emailed asking about this twice but never got a response.

They do not pay you sufficient fuel costs, 25p per mile is below the gov minimum payment of 45p (which even then is too low with fuel prices at £1.40 for petrol, even more for diesel), I drove 3000 miles in one month and only was paid £150 for fuel, they claim the calculations are made via the ACP app but it is a complete lie. I asked for an investigation to be put in and to increase the fuel as a minimum effort and the head office replied saying they won't. 

The main problem with the company however is the absurd amount of time you don't get paid for, for months I had to wait hours in between calls (as was too long and financially impractical to drive to and fro) and was never paid for it. Can have a 10 hour day, assuming you start at 7:30 am one would assume you'd finish at 3:30pm, but no! You don't finish the last client until 9-10pm. A complete joke and immoral. 

I was also scolded by my superior for quite literally 'doing too much' as they said it made other carers who go in and don't do as much as they can to ease the clients lives look bad and can lead to negative cases. Imagine being told off for going above and beyond. 

I also only found out recently that I signed a 0 hour contract, the lady who assisted me in signing the contract asked my availability, to which I told her 20 hours (which was rarely adhered to in all my time working for this company) and she said she can't promise that amount so instead sign the other box which unbeknownst to me meant 0 hours. A complete shambles. 

The only positives were the clients themselves who were usually always a delight, wonderfully interesting, kind and grateful.

I am a very busy individual, juggling school with working and I implore anybody seeking to work in care, especially HomeInstead, to tread carefully when looking for work in care; they take complete advantage. Turned down as I had the wrong address. I would like to mention that I had to give a star in order to proceed with this review. They started superbly .. Home Instead, Sutton Coldfield Franchise. Sutton Coldfield Franchise 
We used Home Instead for 2.5 years to care for my mother before we left them.  I would rank 3 of the 5 carers we experienced as exceptional. They really cared about what they did and did it well. However, the back-office team and owner ranged from adequate to incredibly poor.  Even when things were proven not to work, the way they rigidly and doggedly stuck to their processes and policies was incredibly frustrating for the family trying to manage care needs.  
We tolerated incorrect billing, admin errors and a general coolness towards customer focus. The straw that broke the camel’s back was a bad experience with 1 carer who made fundamental mistakes putting my family member at risk and Home Instead failed to acknowledge this at the management/owner level (or compensate for the fallout charges). When things went wrong of their making, there wasn’t any genuine empathy or goodwill that I experienced. Home Instead are the most expensive that we found, so charging a premium for the hassle and oversight I had to do myself wasn’t acceptable

I spent five months chasing a refund…. I spent five months chasing a refund for a no show by this company.  The care for my mother was respite so only provided while I was on holiday.  She seemed satisfied with the care but obviously I didn’t see it because I was away.  Eventually when I rang to ask for more respite care they booked it in and said they would ring me next day for payment.  No one got back to me so I rang  them to pay and was told the manager had decided they weren’t doing short term respite anymore!  What happened to giving two month’s notice before any changes are made?  I went on the website and what’s the first thing that comes up? “We do respite”!   I think I was singled out as an awkward customer.  I got my mother to write asking for confirmation in writing that the service was withdrawn before she paid another admin fee elsewhere.  Unsurprisingly we heard absolutely nothing.  The company I use now e-mailed me a rota with who was coming when.  What a difference from being abroad to be rung and told the carers haven’t turned up.  I’ve read a lot of the good reviews and wonder whether this company varies from branch to branch around the country?

FOUR weeks charges when stopping on going into full time care. Be aware that this company charge for 4 weeks care after it is stopped.  A charge is reasonable because caregivers have been booked, but FOUR weeks seems very unreasonable.  Care homes are only able to charge for 1 week in same circumstances.  Given the high demand for carers, I am sure they were able to get other work within 4 weeks.  It has been a recent change to their previous T&Cs.  It is hard to pay after a death or unexpected move to full-time care

I was so excited to work for this…. I was so excited to work for this company. The 3 day induction training was unpaid,Saturday Sunday and Monday 9am-4pm. As time went on I received 10hrs but that didn't last long before some clients were taken off me. I was misinformed about claiming car travel allowance so I never received any payments for 3 months and had to arrange a meeting with the main boss to finally receive it. The office staff come across as being supportive but aren't and continually lied to me concerning alot of issues.Never returned my calls. After I'd worked there a short while my client visits were obviously turning into personal care visits which wasn't the role i applied for.I covered holidays for other caregivers and was shocked at the lack of previous care the clients had been receiving. Once I booked a holiday and I received a phone call from the office asking if I needed the holiday as other caregivers that worked more hours than me wanted that day or me having one days holiday 'messed' it up for other caregivers holidays. I finally put in my notice I was told I had to work 4wks notice and then I would receive all monies owed including holiday hours accrued. I never had a contract but worked the 4wks as asked but never received the accrued holiday pay. I contacted the office and as always was told they'd get someone to look into it. Every call was ignored,every message was ignored. 
The caregivers are lovely,the clients are lovely and it was a pleasure spending time with them, but he office staff are not

Visiting Angels or Visiting Demons?. I am writing this from a CAREGIVER'S perspective. I have been a registered nurse for over 25 years, and just signed on with this company for parttime, something to do work. 
In the 6 weeks that I was employed by an Austin Texas franchise owned by Sue Guerrero, I became appalled and frightened at the unprofessionalism practiced first by the franchise owner, SUE GUERRERO, borders upon homicidal.
She hires "caregivers" without having thoroughly vetting them , in some cases not even background checking them or even asking for identification. 
I was shocked that I was hired sight unseen , over the phone, not even having to present my nursing license. I was sent DIRECTLY to a patients home for 4 days in a row before I even met her in person.
I thought that to be suspect.
The other caregivers that I worked with were incompetent to say the least, dangerous in fact. I would NEVER leave one of my loved one's under this company's care.
On the other hand, the first two weeks I was paid fairly promptly, albeit less than what we had agreed upon. I was supposed to be paid 22.00 hourly. My check reflected 14.00 and hour. Mind you , this is with no breaks and no lunch and paying gas and car maintenence.
The second pay period , I received my check 7 days late. Today is May the 6th. Payday was the 1st. 
I have been three times to her office which is 55 minutes away because I was told to PICK UP MY CHECK. 
Each time that I have been there, her office is shut down, locked and she refuses to answer my calls.
I am a very conscientious , punctual individual who cares about her patients. I have done nothing to deserve this treatment.
Please be aware that your caregiver, althouhg you, in good conscious pay Visiting Angels in a timely manner, may not be getting paid...........

Thank you and God Bless

Do Not Trust This Company With Your Health. Twice now, Capsule has delivered Schedule II controlled medication with missing pills. The first time I received 27 pills instead of 30 and, rather than taking responsibility, they insited the mistake was mine and that they could not replace the missing pills.  I explained that I do not need the pills replaced, am meticulous with my medications and that the correct amount had not been delivered, but they essentially shrugged their shoulders.

Two months later, it happened again. This time I received 29 pills instead of 30 and, once again, I was assured that I was mistaken. Refusing to be gaslit a second time, I asked how they could explain tablets that were never delivered. I was told their Pharmacy Manager would contact me.

After 22 days and multiple follow-ups, Pharmacy Manager Mel finally called. He asked me to explain what had happened and, after I spent all of 15 seconds doing so, sat in complete silence. Eventually I asked, “Are you still there?” His response: “Sorry, I didn’t know if I was allowed to speak.”

It felt less like discussing a serious issue with a Pharmacy Manager and more like speaking with a pouting, immature 12-year-old. I jokingly asked whether I should say “over” at the end of each sentence, to which he replied that it “would be very helpful so I know when to speak.” He then offered to transfer my prescription elsewhere. The entire conversation lasted less than 90 seconds.

The lack of professionalism and seriousness on display was astonishing, especially considering we were discussing repeated pill count discrepancies involving a Schedule II controlled substance.

I would strongly urge people not to trust this company with their health. They cannot count to 30, they refuse to take responsibility when errors are raised, and their own Pharmacy Manager lacks the maturity to handle a basic conversation. If they cannot reliably count pills, why should anyone trust them to ensure the correct dosage or even dispense the correct medication?

Most concerning of all, there is still no explanation for the missing tablets. Did Capsule make a counting error? Were there failures in their controls? I still don’t know, because nobody at the company appears interested in finding out. Capsule seemed far more concerned with transferring my prescription elsewhere than investigating why this happened twice.

Your health and wellbeing are too important to place in the hands of people who treat these issues with such alarming indifference

Cancellation without explanation. Extremely frustrating experience with Capsule Pharmacy. I received a notification that my prescription was ready for checkout, which understandably led me to believe the medication was available and my order was moving forward. Less than two hours later, I received the following message:

"Capsule here with an update on your recent order. Unfortunately, your medication is unavailable from our supplier and we do not have an availability date. We cannot transfer this prescription to another pharmacy due to state regulations and recommend you ask your doctor to send this prescription to another pharmacy."
So after telling me my prescription was ready, Capsule canceled the order and essentially left me to start the process over elsewhere. There was no explanation for how a prescription could be marked as ready and then suddenly become unavailable within hours, and no effort to provide an estimated timeline or alternative solution.
The delivery experience is also disappointing. Delivery windows are often long and inconvenient, and same-day delivery is almost never available despite being a major selling point of the service.

Customer service only adds to the frustration. Most communication happens through vague text messages that provide little useful information and make it difficult to get clear answers. When you're dealing with important medications, reliability and transparency matter. Unfortunately, Capsule failed to provide either in this situation.

I would not recommend relying on Capsule for time-sensitive prescriptions

Don't do this to yourself.... I have been using Capsule for over a year now due to my OBGYN office telling me I had to in order to receive the proper discount on my medication. Nope…. I used to love capsule and would recommend it to everyone I knew, BUT NO MORE!

Here are the last three things they did to mess up my last three orders.

1. I needed a medication refill for an upcoming trip. I schedule the delivery a week in advance of my trip because I knew capsule had slowed down a bit recently. Who knew even a week wouldn’t be enough time + I never got the medication. Capsule allowed me to place an order for two days later. Hours before my delivery is supposed to come, they cancel and say they don’t have the medication in stock?? I ask when they will have it and they say they have no clue. Frustrated, I ask for them to transfer it to another pharmacy. I transfer it to a cvs and they say I now have to pay the out of pocket price because capsule already ran my insurance for it and since it’s a controlled substance it can only be filed for insurance every 30 days. So not only did I not get the medication before my trip, I’m still waiting on it since it costs so much without insurance.

2. I have another trip coming up and it requires malaria medication. My doctor sent the prescription and then my travel dates changed which meant I needed more medication. I asked capsule what I should do. 1) ask my doctor for a new prescription or 2) place the first order for 16 pills and then the second for 2 pills. Stunningly they said option 2 and stood by it despite my questioning. I ordered the 16 pills and then went to order the 2. Of course it wouldn’t allow them to be delivered at the same time but even worse, they charged me $15 for the extra two pills??? And the 16 pills were only $20. Unimaginably frustrating. I will concede that they apologized and offered me a half price override, but I still paid a markup for their mistake.

3. I placed an order for antibiotics (which you need to take soon after being prescribed). Luckily, there was a rare same day delivery window. When the delivery window came, I checked the app. They RESCHEDULED my delivery for 24 hours later without notice?? I messaged them and they said the medication was out of stock so they moved it to the next available delivery window, and refused to make an exception to deliver it in the earlier window. If I had known this would happen, I would have gone to pick it up. But of course I don’t learn about it until they have closed and it’s no longer possible to pick it up (at 5 pm I might add).

If you’ve read this far thanks for listening, and I’m sorry you’ve stumbled upon what has become the worst pharmacy. Fool me once, shame on you; fool me twice, shame on me. Fool me three times…✌🏾

It was great until it wasn’t.. This pharmacy was so amazing and we recommended them to anyone who would listen. But unfortunately, we’re telling people the opposite. The last order we made we had to wait for 2 days. My husband was without important meds until then because we couldn’t get the pharmacy changed any sooner than the supposed delivery time slot. It was supposed to be delivered no later than 3pm on Sunday. When 4:00pm rolled around and not even a message was sent, I had to call. The person told me that my order was forgotten at the pharmacy and the location is now closed so we won’t be able to do anything. They did nothing to try and make the delivery urgent or any easier for us. They said to either reschedule or come pick it up. We asked that if the issue wasn’t because we weren’t home, why can’t we be made a priority delivery for next day? They didn’t respond. They said someone would call me and help me out because none of the available delivery windows worked. It’s now Wednesday afternoon and I again had to be the one to reach out. They are having the same attitude and now that I’m asking that the prescription be transferred, they’re again not making it any easier on me. They want me to call the new pharmacy to have them call capsule to get it going. That’s about an hour+ of wait time! I’ve already sent them the phone number and address. We will never be using this service again and will tell everyone that will listen to never use them either

Look Elsewhere!. Selected delivery window two days in advance. Capsule was still unable to deliver my necessary medications. Waited all morning, no delivery, no justifiable excuse.
